name: Power Map
summary: Map who decides, who advises, who blocks, who implements, and who scrutinises.
whenToUseIt: Use this whenever politics feels abstract or personality-driven. It is the fastest way to turn a noisy issue into a structured picture.
steps: List who advises, who can block or amend, and who implements.
Add the scrutiny layer: courts, committees, audits, media, elections.
Mark where the main bottleneck or leverage point actually sits.
example: In Australian housing policy, Cabinet may decide direction, departments build options, the Senate can slow or amend legislation, states shape delivery, and media plus voters determine the political pain tolerance.
